content: DSM-5-TR ASD criteria: Persistent deficits in social communication/interaction (all 3): social-emotional reciprocity deficits, nonverbal communication deficits, relationship development/maintenance deficits. Restricted/repetitive behaviors/interests/activities (≥2): stereotyped/repetitive movements/object use/speech, insistence on sameness/inflexible adherence to routines, highly restricted/fixed interests, sensory hyper/hyporeactivity. Symptoms present in early developmental period. Cause clinically significant impairment. Not better explained by intellectual disability or global developmental delay. Specifiers: with/without intellectual impairment, with/without language impairment, associated with known medical/genetic condition/environmental factor, associated with another neurodevelopmental/mental/behavioral disorder, with catatonia. Severity levels 1-3 based on support needed.
